arts.sum: Barcelona president Joan Laporta appears to leak a RFEF statement about Dani Olmo's La Liga registration.

arts.ttl: Nike to Release No Barcelona 2025 Collection?
arts.sum: From the information we have so far, it seems that Nike will not be releasing a 2025 training collection for Barcelona.

arts.ttl: Barbastro captain hoping for ‘1-0 in the 90th minute’ against Barca
arts.sum: “Yes, I think we are going to enjoy the match. The two teams meet in the competition for the second year running, and Lopez is dreaming of a late winner for the minnows to provide a fairytale win.

arts.ttl: Olmo and Victor still unregistered as Barca’s saga continues
arts.sum: All of the documentation has been sent to La Liga but that has not changed the situation of Olmo and Victor who were deregistered on January 1. Barcelona duo Dani Olmo and Pau Victor remain unregistered as the saga surrounding the two players unfortunately continues.

arts.ttl: Laporta appears to offer update on Olmo’s situation at Barca via WhatsApp
arts.sum: Gerard Romero reports that the Barca chief has, rather bizarrely, offered the following information as part of his WhatsApp status Barcelona president Joan Laporta appears to have offered an update on the current situation regarding Dani Olmo and Pau Victor’s registration in a rather curious way.

arts.ttl: The secret WhatsApp message Barcelona president Laporta deleted about Dani Olmo
arts.sum: The message was lengthy and detailed, explaining that the RFEF (Royal Spanish Football Federation) had agreed with all the arguments presented by FC Barcelona to validate the registrations of two of its players. Shortly after this was revealed, the news broke that LaLiga had confirmed that Barcelona had returned to the 1:1 ratio necessary to sign the players in question.

arts.ttl: Arteta claims Arsenal cannot replicate Barcelona academy due to restrictions
arts.sum: Arteta ‘lived in La Masia’ with Xavi and Iniesta Premier League clubs cannot sign U18s from overseas Mikel Arteta believes it is “impossible” for Arsenal’s academy to produce as many top players as Barcelona’s famous La Masia setup owing to regulations imposed on English clubs. Ethan Nwaneri and Myles Lewis-Skelly have broken into Arsenal’s first team this season after coming through the academy and should feature against Brighton on Saturday. Arteta started his career at Barcelona and shared a dormitory with Xavi Hernández, Andrés Iniesta and Victor Valdés at La Masia. But he suggested he had been frustrated in attempts to recreate the same environment at Arsenal because of certain rules, including restrictions on signing young players from overseas until they are 18.
